tracks.meaning: to go in front of or ahead of someone or something
a person or organization that is the most advanced or successful in a particular field
the opening or introductory part of something that sets the tone or direction
a wire or connection that conducts electrical current in a circuit
a quantity of money lent to a borrower with the expectation of repayment
a naturally occurring mineral or ore from which metal can be extracted
a hint, clue, or piece of information that helps solve a problem or find something
the principal defendant or plaintiff in a legal case
a heavy bluish-gray metal element used in batteries, ammunition, and construction
the foremost position or frontline in a military advance or formation
to be winning or ahead in a game, race, or competition
the main or primary vocal or instrumental part in a musical composition
a metal bar or separator used to space lines of type in printing
a batsman's position or turn to bat in cricket
the principal role or part in a play or performance
